description_long: This is a pair of metal pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, featuring two elongated handles and a set of short, broad jaws. The handles are straight and metallic, showing a matte, slightly worn finish, and are connected by a central pivot joint with visible fasteners. The jaws are thick and slightly curved, with a blunt tip and a serrated gripping surface for enhanced hold on objects. A spring mechanism is visible between the handles, aiding in automatic opening when not squeezed. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for heavy-duty gripping or cutting tasks, commonly found in urban maintenance or repair settings. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A sturdy pair of metal pliers with straight handles, a central pivot, and broad, serrated jaws. The tool has a matte metallic finish and a visible spring mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the jaws and the alignment of the handles, with the spring mechanism partially visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the broad, serrated jaws and the full span of the handles, with the pivot and fasteners clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the jaws and handles, with the spring and fasteners in profile.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the jaws and the open handles, with the spring mechanism and pivot joint visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, metallic handles with a slight ergonomic curve and no visible insulation.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, broad, slightly curved jaws with a serrated gripping surface and blunt tips, suitable for general gripping and cutting.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot with visible fasteners and a spring mechanism for automatic opening; does not appear to be adjustable.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metallic surface with no visible ribbing or rubberized texture.
